project: Influx v1 — PRD 07: Content Extraction (HTML/PDF/article) + Tier 1 & Tier 3 Enrichment
description: Fill in the extraction and enrichment hooks left stubbed by PRDs 04, 05, and 06. This PRD ships HTML extraction (`arxiv.org/html/{id}` and generic web articles via `trafilatura` or `readability-lxml`), PDF extraction (arXiv PDF fallback via `pypdf` / `pdfminer.six`), and the real Tier 1 and Tier 3 enrichment callers using `models.enrich` / `models.extract` in JSON mode against the prompts at `prompts.tier1_enrich` / `prompts.tier3_extract`. The `Tier1Enrichment` and `Tier3Extraction` Pydantic schemas enforce list-length bounds (`contributions ∈ [1,6]`, `claims ∈ [1,10]`, optional list bounds [0,10] for `datasets`, `builds_on`, `open_questions`, `potential_connections`) with a 500-character truncation rule on Tier 3 string elements. Tier selection on initial write replaces the PRD 04 stub: notes that cross the relevance / full_text / deep_extract thresholds ship with `## Summary` (Tier 1), `## Full Text` (Tier 2 = the extracted text body), and `## Claims` / `## Datasets & Benchmarks` / `## Builds On` / `## Open Questions` (Tier 3) populated on first pass — eliminating the seam where a threshold tag was set without its section. Per-tier failure is independent: a note may have a successful Tier 1 + failed Tier 3 and ships with `## Summary`, no Tier 3 sections, and `influx:repair-needed` (FR-ENR-6: no placeholder text on failure). Extraction fall-through is governed by min-length gates (`extraction.min_html_chars` default 1000, `extraction.min_web_chars` default 500) and HTML sanitisation strips `extraction.strip_tags` (default `["script", "iframe", "object", "embed"]`) so no HTML fragments leak into markdown (FR-RES-5). The PRD 06 hooks `re_extract_archive`, `tier2_enrich`, `tier3_extract` are replaced with real implementations imported from `extraction` and `enrich`; PRD 06's hook signatures are unchanged and PRD 06's tests still pass. The `notes.py` renderer is extended to emit `## Full Text` and the four Tier 3 sections in the canonical order (Tier 3 sections precede `## User Notes` per AC-M2-4).

userStories.title: Add Tier1Enrichment Pydantic schema with list-length validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a `Tier1Enrichment` Pydantic schema in `src/influx/schemas.py` so the Tier 1 enrichment caller can validate JSON-mode model output against the contract from FR-ENR-4 (PRD 07 §5.2).
acceptanceCriteria: `Tier1Enrichment` is added to `src/influx/schemas.py` with fields exactly: `contributions: list[str]`, `method: str`, `result: str`, `relevance: str`
`contributions` length is bounded to [1, 6] inclusive — length 0 fails validation, length 7 fails validation, lengths 1 and 6 succeed

`method`, `result`, and `relevance` are stored as `str` fields per FR-ENR-4; any additional validation applied to these fields must remain faithful to the PRD (the PRD only specifies the field types here)
Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_tier1_schema.py` cover positive (length 1, length 6) and negative cases (length 0, length 7) for the `contributions` length bound
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Add Tier3Extraction Pydantic schema with 500-char truncation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a `Tier3Extraction` Pydantic schema in `src/influx/schemas.py` with the per-list bounds and 500-char truncation behaviour from FR-ENR-5 so the Tier 3 caller can validate model output and ingest oversize claims safely (PRD 07 §5.3, AC-07-B, AC-07-C).
acceptanceCriteria: `Tier3Extraction` is added to `src/influx/schemas.py` with fields exactly: `claims: list[str]`, `datasets: list[str]`, `builds_on: list[str]`, `open_questions: list[str]`, `potential_connections: list[str]`
`claims` length is bounded to [1, 10] inclusive — length 0 fails validation (AC-07-C), length 11 fails validation, lengths 1 and 10 succeed
`datasets`, `builds_on`, `open_questions`, `potential_connections` lengths are bounded to [0, 10] inclusive — length 11 fails validation, lengths 0 and 10 succeed
Each string element across all five lists must be non-empty after trim — empty/whitespace strings fail validation
String elements exceeding 500 characters are truncated to 500 on ingest (no validation failure) — a 600-char input claim becomes a 500-char stored claim (AC-07-B)
Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_tier3_schema.py` cover positive and negative cases for every bound; `tests/unit/test_tier3_truncation.py` covers the 500-char truncation behaviour
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Validate extraction config schema/defaults (min_html_chars, min_web_chars, strip_tags)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the existing `extraction.min_html_chars`, `extraction.min_web_chars`, and `extraction.strip_tags` config knobs (already declared on `ExtractionConfig` in `src/influx/config.py` and `influx.example.toml`) to have their schema, defaults, and override behaviour locked down so later extraction-module stories (US-006…US-008) can rely on them as the single source of truth for these tunables (PRD 07 §5.1, AC-X-1 extended). This story does NOT cover wiring the knobs into the extraction modules — that is owned by US-006…US-008.
acceptanceCriteria: `extraction.min_html_chars`, `extraction.min_web_chars`, and `extraction.strip_tags` are declared on `ExtractionConfig` in `src/influx/config.py` with defaults 1000, 500, and `["script", "iframe", "object", "embed"]` respectively, and `influx.example.toml` documents the same defaults
A config that overrides each knob to a non-default value validates successfully via the existing config-loading path and the override is reflected on the parsed `ExtractionConfig` instance

Unit test asserts the defaults of `extraction.min_html_chars`, `extraction.min_web_chars`, and `extraction.strip_tags` and asserts that user-supplied overrides flow through to the parsed config object (covering the config side of FR-ENR-2 / FR-ENR-3 / FR-RES-5); this story does not assert anything about the extraction modules themselves
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Validate tier1_enrich and tier3_extract prompts declare required variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the existing `prompts.tier1_enrich` and `prompts.tier3_extract` config entries to ship prompt bodies whose required template variables match FR-ENR-4 / FR-ENR-5 exactly so the Tier 1 / Tier 3 callers can render them deterministically (PRD 07 §5.2, §5.3). The `[prompts]` config keys themselves already exist in `src/influx/config.py` and `influx.example.toml`; this story is about the prompt content / variable contract, not adding new keys.
acceptanceCriteria: The shipped `tier1_enrich` prompt declares its required template variables exactly as `{title}`, `{abstract}`, `{profile_summary}` and passes the existing `src/influx/prompts.py` variable-set check (`validate_prompt_variables`) — no other variables are required

The shipped `tier3_extract` prompt declares its required template variables exactly as `{title}`, `{full_text}` and passes the existing `src/influx/prompts.py` variable-set check (`validate_prompt_variables`) — no other variables are required

Unit tests assert that loading each prompt via `prompts.load_prompt` returns the prompt text and that `prompts.validate_prompt_variables` accepts the prompt for its key (and rejects a tampered prompt missing or adding a variable with `PromptValidationError`); this story covers the shipped prompt content and variable contract — caller-side rendering and end-to-end JSON-mode caller assertions are out of scope and live in US-009 / US-010
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Add trafilatura and pypdf as project dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `trafilatura` (HTML extraction) and `pypdf` (PDF extraction) added to the project's dependency manifest so the extraction modules can import them (PRD 07 §7).
acceptanceCriteria: `trafilatura` (or `readability-lxml`) is added to `pyproject.toml` dependencies and resolvable via the project's package manager (`uv sync` succeeds)
`pypdf` (or `pdfminer.six`) is added to `pyproject.toml` dependencies and resolvable via the project's package manager
The chosen HTML and PDF extraction dependencies import successfully and are usable from the codebase
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Create extraction/html.py with HTML extraction + tag-stripping (FR-ENR-2, FR-RES-5)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/extraction/html.py` with an HTML extraction function that fetches via PRD 02's guarded HTTP client, strips dangerous tags per `extraction.strip_tags`, and rejects output below `extraction.min_html_chars` so the arXiv flow can call it as the first tier (PRD 07 §5.1 FR-ENR-2, §5.5 FR-RES-5, §6 Create).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/extraction/__init__.py` is created (package marker)

`src/influx/extraction/html.py` is created and exposes HTML extraction that fetches via PRD 02's guarded HTTP client, runs the body through the chosen extractor (`trafilatura` or `readability-lxml`), and produces extracted plain text — function signature shape is left to the implementer

Tags listed in `extraction.strip_tags` (default `["script", "iframe", "object", "embed"]`) are removed from the input HTML before extraction so their contents do not appear in the output text — verified against `tests/fixtures/extraction/*.html` containing each tag (AC-07-F)

When the extracted text length is < `extraction.min_html_chars` (default 1000) the result is rejected so the arXiv flow can fall through to PDF (FR-ENR-2, AC-07-E with 800-char fixture); the exact rejection-signaling mechanism (return sentinel, typed result, raised exception, etc.) is left to the implementer
On HTTP failure or extractor exception the caller can fall through to the next tier — no silent swallow
The output contains no HTML fragments — clean text only (FR-RES-5)
`tests/unit/test_html_extraction.py` covers: success ≥ min_html_chars, rejection < min_html_chars, tag-stripping for each default-stripped tag, no HTML fragments in output
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Create extraction/pdf.py with PDF text extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/extraction/pdf.py` with a PDF-to-text function so the arXiv flow can fall through to PDF extraction when HTML fails or yields below `extraction.min_html_chars` (PRD 07 §5.1 FR-ENR-1, §6 Create, AC-M2-2).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/extraction/pdf.py` is created and exposes PDF-to-text extraction using `pypdf` (or `pdfminer.six`); function signature shape and accepted source forms (URL, local path, bytes) are left to the implementer as long as the arXiv PDF fallback can drive it

Where the PDF fallback path performs an HTTP fetch, that fetch goes through PRD 02's guarded HTTP client somewhere on the call chain (e.g. in a caller, a storage helper, or `extraction/pdf.py` itself); `extraction/pdf.py` itself is not required to own URL fetching, and re-extraction of stored archive files (per PRD 06) does not perform any HTTP fetch
On extractor exception or empty extraction the caller can fall through to abstract-only — no silent swallow; the exact failure-signaling mechanism is left to the implementer
Output is plain text with no PDF binary artifacts or stream markers leaking through

`tests/unit/test_pdf_extraction.py` covers: success path against a recorded fixture in `tests/fixtures/extraction/*.pdf`, extractor failure surfaces as a recognisable failure to the caller, output is plain text only
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Create extraction/article.py with generic web article extraction (FR-ENR-3)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `src/influx/extraction/article.py` with a generic web-article extraction function so future RSS / web sources can extract article text and fall through to feed summary when below `extraction.min_web_chars` (PRD 07 §5.1 FR-ENR-3, §6 Create).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/extraction/article.py` is created and exposes generic web-article extraction that fetches via PRD 02's guarded HTTP client, extracts article body text, and applies the same `extraction.strip_tags` sanitisation as `extraction/html.py` — function signature shape is left to the implementer

When the extracted text length is < `extraction.min_web_chars` (default 500) the result is rejected so the caller can fall through to feed summary (FR-ENR-3); the exact rejection-signaling mechanism is left to the implementer
On HTTP failure or extractor exception the caller can fall through — no silent swallow
The output contains no HTML fragments — clean text only (FR-RES-5)
`tests/unit/test_article_extraction.py` covers: success ≥ min_web_chars, rejection < min_web_chars, tag-stripping, no HTML fragments in output
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Replace enrich.py Tier 1 stub with real tier1_enrich implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `enrich.tier1_enrich` in `src/influx/enrich.py` to invoke the configured JSON-mode enrichment model slot against `prompts.tier1_enrich` and validate the response with `Tier1Enrichment`, replacing the PRD 04 stub (PRD 07 §5.2 FR-ENR-4).
acceptanceCriteria: `tier1_enrich(title, abstract, profile_summary, ...)` in `src/influx/enrich.py` renders the prompt loaded from `prompts.tier1_enrich` (US-004) with the exact template variables `{title}`, `{abstract}`, `{profile_summary}` and dispatches it to the configured JSON-mode enrichment model slot — the slot is read as `models.enrich` from `AppConfig` (per the `src/influx/config.py` `models` configuration shape); a new or existing runtime inference helper may be used, but it MUST resolve the model slot from the runtime config rather than hardcoding any provider/model identifier

The JSON response is parsed and validated against the `Tier1Enrichment` schema (US-001); validation failure surfaces in a way the caller can handle per FR-ENR-6 (the specific error shape is left to the implementer)
The PRD 04 stub behaviour (always-empty / always-stub Tier 1) is removed — `enrich.py` no longer returns the stub value

Unit tests cover: successful enrichment returns a valid `Tier1Enrichment`, validation failure surfaces in a caller-handleable way (with `contributions: []` triggering AC-07-A path), prompt is rendered with all three required variables, and the configured JSON-mode enrichment model slot is the one invoked (verified by injecting a fake model client / inference helper)
Existing PRD 04 tests that depended on the stub are updated or deleted as appropriate (preserve coverage of behaviours, drop coverage of the stub itself)
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Add tier3_extract to enrich.py with truncation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `enrich.tier3_extract` in `src/influx/enrich.py` to invoke the configured JSON-mode extraction model slot against `prompts.tier3_extract` and validate the response with `Tier3Extraction` so deep extraction is callable on initial write and via PRD 06's repair sweep (PRD 07 §5.3 FR-ENR-5).
acceptanceCriteria: `tier3_extract(title, full_text, ...)` in `src/influx/enrich.py` renders the prompt loaded from `prompts.tier3_extract` (US-004) with the exact template variables `{title}`, `{full_text}` and dispatches it to the configured JSON-mode extraction model slot (the model slot is read from the runtime config)
The JSON response is parsed and validated against the `Tier3Extraction` schema (US-002); list-bound violations surface in a way the caller can handle per FR-ENR-6 (AC-07-C)
Oversize string elements (> 500 chars) are truncated to 500 on ingest without raising (AC-07-B)

Unit tests cover: successful extraction returns a valid `Tier3Extraction`, `claims: []` triggers validation failure, a 600-char claim is truncated to 500, prompt is rendered with both required variables, and the configured JSON-mode extraction model slot is the one invoked (verified by injecting a fake model client / inference helper)
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Render ## Full Text section in notes.py (Tier 2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `notes.py` to render the `## Full Text` section using the extracted Tier 2 text body so notes that cross the `full_text` threshold ship with the section populated on first pass (PRD 07 §1, §5.6, AC-M2-1).
acceptanceCriteria: The canonical note renderer in `src/influx/notes.py` accepts a Tier 2 full-text input (extracted plain text) and emits a `## Full Text` section with that text as the body
When the Tier 2 input is absent or empty (extraction failed or below threshold) the renderer omits the `## Full Text` section entirely (no placeholder, no empty heading) per FR-ENR-6

`## Full Text` is rendered in the Influx-owned body before `## User Notes`; existing renderer ordering is preserved and this story does not prescribe a more specific absolute position beyond what FR-NOTE-9 / master PRD §8.1 already requires
Unit tests cover: section rendered when text provided, section omitted when text absent, ordering preserved
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Render Tier 3 sections (Claims / Datasets & Benchmarks / Builds On / Open Questions) in notes.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `notes.py` to render the four Tier 3 sections from a `Tier3Extraction` so notes that cross the `deep_extract` threshold ship with all four sections on first pass, ordered before `## User Notes` (PRD 07 §5.3, AC-M2-4).
acceptanceCriteria: The canonical note renderer in `src/influx/notes.py` accepts a `Tier3Extraction` input and emits `## Claims`, `## Datasets & Benchmarks`, `## Builds On`, `## Open Questions` sections (one bullet per item)
All four Tier 3 sections appear BEFORE `## User Notes` (AC-M2-4) and follow the canonical order from FR-NOTE-9 / master PRD §8.1
When the Tier 3 input is absent (extraction failed) the renderer omits ALL four sections entirely — no placeholder, no empty headings (FR-ENR-6)
`potential_connections` is NOT rendered into a body section (consumed by PRD 08 LCMA only)

Unit tests cover: all four sections rendered when extraction provided, all omitted when extraction absent, ordering correct (sections before `## User Notes`), `potential_connections` not rendered as a section
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Render ## Summary section from Tier1Enrichment in notes.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `notes.py` to render the `## Summary` section from a `Tier1Enrichment` as a structured block (Contributions list + Method/Result/Relevance paragraphs) so notes that cross the `relevance` threshold ship with `## Summary` populated on first pass (PRD 07 §5.2 FR-ENR-4).
acceptanceCriteria: The canonical note renderer in `src/influx/notes.py` accepts a `Tier1Enrichment` input and emits `## Summary` containing `### Contributions` (bullet list of contributions), then `### Method`, `### Result`, `### Relevance` paragraphs in that order
When the Tier1Enrichment input is absent (Tier 1 enrichment failed) the renderer omits the `## Summary` section entirely — no placeholder text (FR-ENR-6, AC-07-A)
Section ordering: `## Summary` follows the canonical order from FR-NOTE-9 / master PRD §8.1 (existing renderer order is preserved)
Unit tests cover: section rendered with all four sub-blocks when Tier1Enrichment provided, section omitted when absent, sub-block ordering correct
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Wire initial-write extraction (HTML → PDF → abstract-only) for arXiv items on the first-write pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the arXiv initial-write pipeline (rooted in `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` per PRD 07 §6 Modify) to invoke `extraction.html` then `extraction.pdf` then fall back to abstract-only when a candidate's score crosses the `full_text` threshold, so notes ship with their Tier 2 sections populated on first pass (PRD 07 §5.1 FR-ENR-1, §5.6, AC-M2-1, AC-M2-2, AC-M2-3, AC-07-E). Placement of the extraction call site between the arXiv provider and any orchestration layer is left to the implementer.
acceptanceCriteria: On initial write, every arXiv note has its `text:*` tier tag selected by the extraction outcome (`text:html` on HTML success, `text:pdf` on PDF fallback success, `text:abstract-only` when both fail), replacing the always-`text:abstract-only` stub from PRD 04 (PRD 07 §1, §5.6); the precise placement of the extraction call site (provider, orchestration, or a helper between them) is left to the implementer

The arXiv extraction order on initial write is HTML (`arxiv.org/html/{id}`) → PDF → abstract-only per FR-ENR-1, with HTML rejected when extracted length < `extraction.min_html_chars` (AC-07-E) so the pipeline falls through to PDF

When the candidate's score crosses the `full_text` threshold AND HTML extraction succeeds (≥ `extraction.min_html_chars`), `## Full Text` carries the extracted text and the `full-text` tag is set; tier tag is `text:html` (AC-M2-1)

When the candidate's score crosses the `full_text` threshold AND HTML extraction fails or yields below `min_html_chars` but PDF extraction succeeds, `## Full Text` carries the PDF-extracted text and the `full-text` tag is set; tier tag is `text:pdf` (AC-M2-2, AC-07-E)

When the candidate's score crosses the `full_text` threshold AND both HTML and PDF extraction fail, the note is written with no `## Full Text` section, no `full-text` tag, tier tag `text:abstract-only`, and is tagged `influx:repair-needed` per FR-ENR-6 / §5.6 so PRD 06's repair sweep can retry; `influx:text-terminal` is NOT added at this initial-write point (AC-M2-3)

When the candidate's score is below the `full_text` threshold, `## Full Text` is NOT rendered and the `full-text` tag is NOT set, regardless of which extraction tier produced the text — preserving the section-iff-tag invariant gated on the threshold

The arXiv first-write path exercises the real HTML → PDF → abstract-only extraction stack defined by this story rather than the PRD 04 stub; the precise wiring (which module owns the call site, how the source-agnostic `item_provider` seam from PRD 04 is satisfied, etc.) is left to the implementer as long as `scheduler.run_profile` for an arXiv profile drives this extraction stack end-to-end
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Wire initial-write Tier 1 + Tier 3 enrichment on the arXiv first-write pipeline with per-tier failure independence (FR-ENR-6)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the arXiv initial-write pipeline (rooted in `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` per PRD 07 §6 Modify) to call `enrich.tier1_enrich` when the relevance threshold is crossed and `enrich.tier3_extract` when the deep_extract threshold is crossed before the first write, with each tier's failure handled independently per FR-ENR-6 (PRD 07 §5.6, AC-07-A, AC-07-D). Tag derivation (`full-text`, `influx:deep-extracted`) is set on the write path so the PRD 04 'tag without section' seam is removed. Placement of the enrichment call sites between the arXiv provider and any orchestration layer is left to the implementer.
acceptanceCriteria: When score ≥ `relevance` threshold the initial-write pipeline calls `enrich.tier1_enrich` before the first write and the resulting `Tier1Enrichment` is rendered into `## Summary` (US-013); on failure the note is written WITHOUT `## Summary` and tagged `influx:repair-needed` (AC-07-A)

When score ≥ `deep_extract` threshold the initial-write pipeline calls `enrich.tier3_extract` against the extracted full text before the first write and the resulting `Tier3Extraction` is rendered into the four Tier 3 sections (US-012); on failure the note is written WITHOUT Tier 3 sections and tagged `influx:repair-needed` (AC-07-C)

Per-tier failure independence: a Tier 1 success + Tier 3 failure produces a note with `## Summary`, no Tier 3 sections, `influx:repair-needed` (AC-07-D); other combinations follow the same independence rule
No placeholder text is inserted on enrichment failure — failed sections are simply absent from the note body (FR-ENR-6)

Tag derivation on the initial-write path enforces the section-iff-tag invariant: `full-text` is set iff the rendered note carries a non-empty `## Full Text` body; `influx:deep-extracted` is set iff all four Tier 3 sections exist (no tag without section, removing the PRD 04 seam at the write path)
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Wire production-default repair hooks for re_extract_archive, tier2_enrich, tier3_extract that preserve PRD 06 signatures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want production-default repair hook implementations wired into the PRD 06 sweep that preserve the PRD 06 live hook signatures (`re_extract_archive(note, archive_path) -> ReExtractionResult`, `tier2_enrich(note) -> None`, `tier3_extract(note) -> None` operating on the mutable note dict — see `src/influx/repair.py:136` and `src/influx/repair.py:174`) and internally call the lower-level extraction / enrichment helpers from US-006…US-010 (whose own signatures, e.g. `tier3_extract(title, full_text, ...)` from US-010, do NOT match the PRD 06 hook signatures and therefore cannot be used directly as `SweepHooks` defaults). The `SweepHooks` test-injection seam is preserved unchanged. The shape and naming of the production defaults (module-level functions, methods, factories, etc.) is left to the implementer (PRD 07 §6 Modify, AC-07-G).
acceptanceCriteria: Production-default repair hook implementations are wired in for `re_extract_archive`, `tier2_enrich`, and `tier3_extract` that conform exactly to the PRD 06 `ReExtractArchiveHook(note, archive_path) -> ReExtractionResult`, `Tier2EnrichHook(note) -> None`, `Tier3ExtractHook(note) -> None` signatures and operate on the mutable note dict — the specific shape (module-level functions, methods, factories, etc.) and any naming/exporting decisions are left to the implementer

Each production default internally calls the lower-level helpers from this PRD: the `re_extract_archive` default re-extracts from the stored archive artifact using `extraction.html` or `extraction.pdf` as appropriate to the archived artifact type (per AC-07-G — the source PRD lists both extractors as valid re-extraction paths) and returns a `ReExtractionResult` discriminating Upgrade / Terminal / Transient per PRD 06 §5.2; the `tier3_extract` default calls `enrich.tier3_extract` (US-010) and applies the result back onto the mutable note (sections + tags) so the sweep's rewrite carries `influx:deep-extracted` on success

A production-default Tier 2 implementation is provided that, given a note dict, drives Tier 2 (`## Full Text`) re-population from the stored archive (or whatever Tier 2 source is appropriate after this PRD's extraction stack lands) and applies the result back onto the mutable note so the sweep's rewrite carries `full-text` on success — this is the wiring promised by PRD 06's `Tier2EnrichHook(note) -> None` contract

The `SweepHooks` injection seam from PRD 06 is preserved unchanged: test injection of fake hooks via `SweepHooks` continues to work, and the real implementations become the production defaults at the sweep entrypoint
`re_extract_archive` operates against the stored archive artifact, not a live HTTP fetch (the archive is the source of truth for re-extraction per AC-07-G)

All PRD 06 behaviors covered by integration tests (Upgrade / Terminal / Transient outcomes; chronic content_too_large exemption; sweep idempotency; rejection authority) remain green with the real implementations wired in as defaults; the PRD 06 hook signatures and the `SweepHooks` injection seam are preserved unchanged, but targeted test updates are permitted where wiring real defaults into the sweep requires them, provided the tests still cover the same PRD 06 behaviors
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

Test passes via `pytest`

userStories.title: Integration test: arXiv abstract-only initial write (no influx:text-terminal)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test asserting that abstract-only initial writes do NOT carry `influx:text-terminal` so AC-M2-3 (full) is provably satisfied (PRD 07 §9, AC-M2-3).
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/integration/test_arxiv_abstract_only_initial_write.py` is created
Test seeds an arXiv candidate that crosses the `full_text` threshold and where both HTML and PDF extraction fail
Test asserts the canonical note carries `text:abstract-only`, has NO `## Full Text` section, and has NO Tier 3 sections
Test asserts the note IS tagged `influx:repair-needed` per FR-ENR-6 / §5.6 so the note enters PRD 06's repair sweep on a subsequent run
Test asserts the note does NOT carry `influx:text-terminal` at this initial-write point (terminality is established only by PRD 06's subsequent successful re-extraction sweep)
Test passes via `pytest`
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Integration test: per-tier failure independence (AC-07-D)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test that drives Tier 1 success + Tier 3 failure to assert per-tier independence so AC-07-D is provably satisfied (PRD 07 §9, AC-07-D).
acceptanceCriteria: An integration test is created (e.g. `tests/integration/test_per_tier_failure_independence.py`) that seeds a candidate where Tier 1 enrichment succeeds and Tier 3 extraction fails
Test asserts the resulting note carries `## Summary` populated from the Tier 1 result, has NO Tier 3 sections, and is tagged `influx:repair-needed`
Test asserts that on a subsequent PRD 06 repair sweep run with a now-succeeding Tier 3 stub, only Tier 3 is retried and the note ends with all sections populated and `influx:repair-needed` cleared
Test passes via `pytest`
Typecheck passes (`uv run pyright`)

userStories.title: Quality gates: ruff + pyright + pytest green; coverage ≥ 80% on extraction/*.py and enrich.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full quality bar from PRD 07 §10 Definition of Done to be green so the PRD can be merged (PRD 07 §10).
acceptanceCriteria: The repo's Ruff lint and format checks pass via the repo's defined lint gate (`make lint`, equivalent to `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` and `uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/`)
The repo's Pyright typecheck passes via the repo's defined typecheck gate (`make typecheck`, equivalent to `uv run pyright`)
`pytest` runs the entire suite green, including all new unit and integration tests added by US-001…US-020 AND all PRD 04, 05, 06 tests still pass with the real extraction / enrichment wired in

A dedicated Tier 3 success + render integration test exists (e.g. `tests/integration/test_tier3_success_and_render.py` — added by this story if not contributed by an earlier story) that drives Tier 1 success + Tier 3 success on initial write and asserts the canonical note carries `## Summary`, all four Tier 3 sections (`## Claims`, `## Datasets & Benchmarks`, `## Builds On`, `## Open Questions`) before `## User Notes`, and the `influx:deep-extracted` tag, satisfying the PRD §9 Tests-required 'Tier 3 success + render' scenario and AC-M2-4
Coverage is ≥ 80% on `src/influx/extraction/html.py`, `src/influx/extraction/pdf.py`, `src/influx/extraction/article.py`, and `src/influx/enrich.py`

The PRD 04 'threshold tag without section' seam is verifiably gone: `full-text` tag exists iff `## Full Text` body is non-empty; `influx:deep-extracted` exists iff Tier 3 sections exist (assertion lives in an existing or new test)
No remaining stub modules in the codebase except those owned by PRDs 08, 09, 10
